title: Streptostachys hirsuta
taxonomy: Streptostachys hirsuta is classified in the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the madder family. This family consists of about 600 genera and over 13,000 species. The genus Streptostachys is a small genus of flowering plants and is native to tropical Africa. More specifically, Streptostachys hirsuta is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. Its leaves and stems have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat wounds and skin conditions. Additionally, Streptostachys hirsuta may have hepatoprotective properties, helping to protect the liver from damage.
